name: Kumano Kodo Pilgrimage
description: Walking the ancient pilgrimage trails of the Kii Peninsula, following footsteps of emperors and monks through misty mountain forests.

waypoints.title: Takijiri-oji
waypoints.blog: The sacred entrance to the Kumano Kodo. A tall torii gate marks the beginning of the trail, standing guard over centuries of pilgrimage.

waypoints.title: Mountain Forest
waypoints.blog: Ancient cedar trees tower overhead, their trunks wrapped in moss. The trail winds upward through a cathedral of green.

waypoints.title: Takahara Village
waypoints.sectionTitle: Midday: The Village in the Clouds
waypoints.blog: Known as the *village in the mist*, Takahara sits perched on a ridge with sweeping views of the valley below. We stopped for onigiri and green tea at a small rest house.

waypoints.title: Descent Trail
waypoints.blog: The afternoon descent passes through bamboo groves and across small wooden bridges. Every turn reveals another view of the misty mountains.

waypoints.title: Chikatsuyu
waypoints.blog: The trail ends in the small village of Chikatsuyu, where we soaked in a riverside onsen as the sun set behind the mountains.
